Note: Note: On June 22nd there is  note at 0500 that the barometer is broken. This may explain these jumps, or it may just be this sloppy log-keeper.
1600: The baro value of 30.03 is bad. I changed it to 30.30.
2400: The baro value of 30.10 is bad. I changed it yo 30.50. North winds force 4, pressures should be rising.
0400: Another bad baro value. I changed 30.35 it to 30.53.
2000: This temperature of 11 looks wrong. Maybe 21.
0800: This temperature of 8 looks too low, given a moderate north wind overnight. I changed it to 28.
0000: He calls it a blalzing gale. I coded it as a fresh gale.
Note the jump in dates. No weather data logged while the ship was in drydock.

Other: 1400: The ship came out of drydock.

Note: The next five baro values of 29.40 are too probably low. I have no idea of what to do with them.
1400: Passed Craney Island. Posn: 36.89, -76.33. Made 7.5 miles in 31 minutes.
He seems to be doing speed trials. No explicit nav data are logged. This is just a guess as to where he went.
0600: I think this water temp should be 54 or the previous one 45. I'm beginning to think this log-writer is dyslexic. I left it at 45.

0-900: A later editor crossed out the baro readings of 30.22 and 30.24. I left them in. He also crossed out the water temp of 81 at 0400. I have no idea why he crossed these out, unless he has access to some other log, but he didn't inut any corrected value.
